payers.name: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts
payers.productName: Standard Control with Advanced Control Specialty Formulary (CVS Caremark)

classes.name: Estrogen — transdermal
classes.description: Transdermal 17β-estradiol (patch, gel, or spray); skips first-pass hepatic metabolism, lower VTE risk than oral.
classes.plainDescription: An estrogen patch or gel absorbed through the skin — gentler on clot risk than pills.

classes.name: Estrogen — oral
classes.description: Oral estradiol or conjugated estrogens; effective, but first-pass hepatic metabolism raises VTE/clot and stroke risk vs transdermal.
classes.plainDescription: An estrogen pill taken by mouth. Works well, but carries a higher clot risk than the patch.

classes.name: Progestogen
classes.description: Progestogen added to systemic estrogen for endometrial protection in patients with an intact uterus; micronized progesterone preferred.
classes.plainDescription: Added to estrogen if you still have a uterus, to protect the uterine lining.

classes.name: Vaginal (local) estrogen
classes.description: Low-dose vaginal estradiol (cream, tablet, insert, or ring) for genitourinary syndrome of menopause; minimal systemic absorption.
classes.plainDescription: Low-dose estrogen placed vaginally for dryness and urinary symptoms (GSM).

classes.name: Estrogen–progestin (oral combo)
classes.description: A single oral tablet with both an estrogen and a progestin, for a person with an intact uterus; generic estradiol/norethindrone acetate is the usual first-pass.
classes.plainDescription: One pill that combines estrogen and the uterus-protecting hormone.

paRequired.reason: Brand-preferred-over-generic (BOGL) product; PA required per the "BP" marker interacting with the brand-vs-generic PA rule for this specific branded patch

records.tier: No explicit dollar/tier language in MHDL; generic estradiol transdermal patch is covered without PA and carries mandatory 90-day supply (M90) or allowable 90-day (A90) designation depending on the specific product.
records.verificationNote: Read directly from the MassHealth Drug List (MHDL) A-Z drug list, therapeutic class "HORMONE REPLACEMENT THERAPY," accessed via the alphabetic index pages (pubdruglist.do?index=E and index=V) in the current session (2026-07-07). Confirmed generic estradiol transdermal patch entries (Alora, Climara, Divigel, generic gel, Elestrin, Estring, Evamist, Femring, Menostar, Minivelle) show no PA symbol, only M90/A90 supply designations, while Vivelle-Dot specifically shows "BP, M90" (brand preferred over generic). No dedicated PA-policy detail page exists for this class in the MHDL therapeutic-table list (Hormone Replacement Therapy is not among the ~84 numbered tables), consistent with it being a largely non-restricted class; this is inferred from the absence of any HRT-specific entry in the site's PA-forms directory (pubpa.do) combined with direct confirmation that individual patch products carry no PA marker. The 0.05mg twice-weekly strength/sig is a standard representative dose, not verbatim from the MHDL page (which documents coverage/PA status by product, not detailed dosing).

preferredAgent.sig: Take 1 tablet (1mg) by mouth once daily
preferredAgent.plainSig: Take one 1mg tablet by mouth once a day.

records.preferredRestriction: The generic estradiol tablet itself shows "M90, #" in the A-Z list, where "#" indicates PA is required for the brand-name version specifically (an FDA A-rated generic equivalent exists) — the generic tablet fills without this restriction.

alternatives.note: Brand preferred over generic (BOGL), A90 allowed, no PA symbol on the tablet form
Covered without PA or BP marker

paRequired.reason: Brand requires PA per the "#" FDA A-rated generic equivalent marker; generic estradiol tablet is not restricted

records.tier: No explicit dollar/tier language in MHDL; generic oral estradiol tablet is covered without PA restriction (M90 mandatory 90-day supply); conjugated estrogens (Premarin) tablet is brand-preferred-over-generic with A90 allowable 90-day supply.
records.verificationNote: Read directly from the MassHealth Drug List (MHDL) A-Z drug list, therapeutic class "HORMONE REPLACEMENT THERAPY," accessed via pubdruglist.do?index=E (estradiol tablet: "M90, #") and index=P (Premarin tablet: "A90, BP"; Premarin cream/vial: no symbols) in the current session (2026-07-07). The "#" marker on generic estradiol tablet-Estrace confirms PA applies only to the branded Estrace product per MassHealth's standard brand-vs-generic rule, not to generic estradiol itself. As with the transdermal class, no dedicated therapeutic-table PA-policy page exists for Hormone Replacement Therapy in the MHDL's numbered table list; this class-level absence of a PA form was cross-checked against the full PA-forms directory (pubpa.do), which lists ~80 categories and does not include one for HRT/estrogen. The 1mg tablet strength/sig is a standard representative oral estradiol dose, not verbatim page text.

records.tier: No explicit dollar/tier language in MHDL; generic micronized progesterone capsule and generic medroxyprogesterone/norethindrone acetate tablets are covered without PA.
records.verificationNote: Read directly from the MassHealth Drug List (MHDL) A-Z drug list, therapeutic class "HORMONE REPLACEMENT THERAPY," accessed via pubdruglist.do?index=P (Prometrium: "A90, #"; Provera: "A90, #") and pubdruglist.do?index=A (Aygestin/norethindrone: A90, no PA symbol) in the current session (2026-07-07). Also checked the MHDL's numbered "Progesterone Agents" therapeutic table (Table 70, reached via pubtheradetail.do?id=253) and confirmed that table covers ONLY obstetric/pregnancy indications (preterm birth prevention, miscarriage prevention, secondary amenorrhea progestin-challenge) for progesterone gel (Crinone) and vaginal insert (Endometrin) — it does not address menopausal HRT endometrial-protection use, which instead lives under the separate "HORMONE REPLACEMENT THERAPY" A-Z class with generic progesterone/medroxyprogesterone/norethindrone acetate covered without PA (only brand names carry the "#" PA marker). This class distinction (pregnancy-indication progesterone vs. HRT-indication progestogen) is directly confirmed, not inferred.

Estring (vaginal ring) appeared in the same E-index fetch listed among HRT-class estradiol brand variants without an explicit PA symbol captured in that pass — its no-PA status is inferred by pattern consistency with other non-oral/non-tablet estradiol delivery forms in the same list rather than independently re-confirmed with its own row read, so that specific alternative is marked partial-strength evidence within an otherwise verified class. No dedicated PA-policy detail table exists for vaginal estrogen in the MHDL's numbered therapeutic-table list, consistent with the class being non-restricted at the generic level.

records.tier: No explicit dollar/tier language in MHDL; the generic estradiol/norethindrone acetate tablet and most other oral estrogen-progestin combination brands (Combipatch, Prefest, Angeliq, Premphase, Prempro) are covered without PA; only Activella brand and Bijuva require PA.
records.verificationNote: Read directly from the MassHealth Drug List (MHDL) A-Z drug list, therapeutic class "HORMONE REPLACEMENT THERAPY," accessed via pubdruglist.do?index=E (estradiol/norethindrone-Activella generic: "M90, #"; estradiol/norethindrone-Combipatch: no PA symbol; estradiol/norgestimate: no PA symbol) and index=P (Premphase, Prempro, Prefest: no PA symbols) and index=B (Bijuva: "PA" with paId=21 link) in the current session (2026-07-07). Confirmed the generic estradiol/norethindrone acetate combination tablet (marketed generically and as Mimvey, per WebSearch cross-check against Teva/GoodRx product pages, though the MHDL row itself does not name Mimvey specifically) is the practical first-pass oral combo pick, filling without PA, while the Activella brand name and the newer bioidentical Bijuva combination require PA. No dedicated PA-policy detail table exists for this class in the MHDL's numbered therapeutic-table list, consistent with oral combination HRT being a largely non-restricted class apart from Activella/Bijuva brand-specific PA.

records.verificationNote: Read directly from 'Hormonal Agents - Sex Hormones and Birth Control' (PDF pages 108-131, effective Jan. 1, 2026, 'Last Update: 06/15/26'). Generic 'estradiol transdermal patch twice weekly' and 'estradiol transdermal patch weekly' rows are both Tier 1 with no PA/ST/QL notation. Generic 'estradiol transdermal gel' (two strength bands) is Tier 2 and Tier 3 respectively, no PA. Brand-name patches ALORA, CLIMARA, VIVELLE-DOT, and MINIVELLE are each individually confirmed Tier 3 with no PA/ST flag printed (i.e., covered but at the least-favorable cost-sharing tier, correctly classified as non-preferred rather than PA-blocked); CLIMARA PRO (the estrogen-progestin combination patch) is the one exception at Tier 2. Note: because CLIMARA/VIVELLE-DOT/MINIVELLE/ALORA carry no PA/ST flag in the LIMITATIONS column, they are technically 'covered, just at a higher tier' — the paRequired classification above reflects that these are the least-preferred/highest-cost tier for this class, distinct from a true PA/step barrier; treat with that nuance if displaying to users (they are not blocked, just Tier 3).

records.verificationNote: Read directly from 'Hormonal Agents - Sex Hormones and Birth Control' (PDF pages 108-112, effective Jan. 1, 2026, 'Last Update: 06/15/26'). Generic 'estradiol oral tablet' (0.5/1/2 mg) is Tier 1 with no PA/ST/QL flag. 'estrogens conjugated oral tablet' (generic Premarin strengths) is Tier 2, no PA. Brand PREMARIN ORAL TABLET is also Tier 2 (matching its generic's tier, so no meaningful brand penalty beyond standard tiering), and DUAVEE (conjugated estrogens/bazedoxifene, no generic) is Tier 3. No PA/ST flags were found on any oral estrogen row in this document.

records.verificationNote: Read directly from 'Hormonal Agents - Sex Hormones and Birth Control' (PDF pages 110-113, effective Jan. 1, 2026, 'Last Update: 06/15/26'). Generic 'progesterone oral capsule' (100/200 mg, i.e. micronized progesterone/generic Prometrium) is Tier 1 with no PA/ST/QL flag. 'medroxyprogesterone acetate oral tablet' (generic Provera) and 'norethindrone acetate oral tablet' are also both Tier 1, no PA. The only restricted item found in this class is the vaginal progesterone insert, which is Tier 3 and gated to infertility-benefit plans specifically (not the endometrial-protection indication being sourced here).

'yuvafem vaginal tablet 10 mcg' (the branded-generic version of Vagifem) is also Tier 1. ESTRING (estradiol vaginal ring) is Tier 2, no PA. records.verificationNote: Read directly off the 'Hormonal Agents - Sex Hormones and Birth Control' section (extracted text lines ~9306-10169) of the HPHC Select 3-Tier 2026 Prescription Drug List. Both generic 'estradiol transdermal patch twice weekly' and 'estradiol transdermal patch weekly' lines are Tier 1 with only 'HSA' -- no PA/ST. Named generic equivalents 'dotti transdermal patch' and 'lyllana transdermal patch' (twice-weekly, matching Vivelle-Dot dosing) are also Tier 1. The generic 'estradiol transdermal gel' (standard strengths) is Tier 2; a higher-concentration 0.06% gel variant is Tier 3. All confirmed brand-name patches (VIVELLE-DOT, MINIVELLE, CLIMARA, ALORA) are Tier 3; CLIMARA PRO (combo patch) is Tier 2. None of these branded items carries a PA/ST/non-formulary flag in the notes column -- they are simply non-preferred (higher tier but still covered), so per task instructions they are listed under alternatives (not paRequired, which is reserved for true PA/step/non-formulary outcomes -- none apply to this class). Cover page: 'Created: October 1, 2025'; content pages: 'Last Update: 06/15/26'.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the 'Hormonal Agents - Sex Hormones and Birth Control' section (extracted text lines ~9306-10169) of the HPHC Select 3-Tier 2026 Prescription Drug List. Generic 'estradiol oral tablet 0.5 mg, 1 mg, 2 mg' is Tier 1 with no notes at all (no PA/ST/HSA flag) -- the cleanest possible coverage status. 'estrogens conjugated oral tablet' (generic Premarin, 0.3-1.25 mg strengths) and brand 'PREMARIN ORAL TABLET' are both Tier 2 with 'HSA' note only, no PA. DUAVEE (conjugated estrogens/bazedoxifene) is Tier 3, no PA. Targeted string search confirmed ESTRACE (branded oral estradiol) does not appear anywhere in the document -- absent, meaning the generic fully substitutes for it, consistent with typical PBM practice. Cover page: 'Created: October 1, 2025'; content pages: 'Last Update: 06/15/26'.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the 'Hormonal Agents - Sex Hormones and Birth Control' section (extracted text lines ~9306-10169) of the HPHC Select 3-Tier 2026 Prescription Drug List. 'progesterone oral capsule 100 mg, 200 mg' is Tier 1 with no PA/ST notes. 'medroxyprogesterone acetate oral tablet 10 mg, 2.5 mg, 5 mg' and 'norethindrone acetate oral tablet 5 mg' are both also Tier 1, no PA. Brand PROMETRIUM does not appear anywhere in the document (targeted search confirmed absent) -- the generic micronized progesterone capsule fully substitutes. Micronized progesterone (generic Prometrium) was selected as preferred per current clinical guidance (Menopause Society) favoring it over synthetic progestins for endometrial protection, and the formulary itself places it at the lowest tier with no restriction, consistent with that preference. Cover page: 'Created: October 1, 2025'; content pages: 'Last Update: 06/15/26'.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the 'Hormonal Agents - Sex Hormones and Birth Control' section (extracted text lines ~9306-10169) of the HPHC Select 3-Tier 2026 Prescription Drug List. 'estradiol vaginal cream 0.01 %' and 'estradiol vaginal tablet 10 mcg' are both Tier 1 with no PA/ST notes. 'yuvafem vaginal tablet 10 mcg' (generic Vagifem equivalent) is also Tier 1, no PA. Brand ESTRING (vaginal ring) is Tier 2, no PA. PREMARIN VAGINAL CREAM is Tier 2, no PA. IMVEXXY (vaginal insert, starter and maintenance packs) is Tier 3, no PA. Brand VAGIFEM does not appear in the document (confirmed absent via targeted search) -- fully generic-substituted. All forms (cream, tablet, ring, insert) are covered with no restriction; estradiol vaginal cream chosen as representative preferred agent as the lowest-tier, most commonly prescribed generic form. Cover page: 'Created: October 1, 2025'; content pages: 'Last Update: 06/15/26'.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the 'Hormonal Agents - Sex Hormones and Birth Control' section (extracted text lines ~9306-10169) of the HPHC Select 3-Tier 2026 Prescription Drug List. 'estradiol-norethindrone acet oral tablet 0.5-0.1 mg, 1-0.5 mg' (the generic form of Activella, at the exact 1mg/0.5mg strength commonly used as first-line combo HT) is Tier 1 with no PA/ST notes. 'mimvey oral tablet 1-0.5 mg' (another labeled generic at the same strength) is also Tier 1. The ethinyl-estradiol-based combo generics ('jinteli', 'fyavolv', 'norethindrone-eth estradiol') -- generic equivalents of FemHRT -- are all Tier 1, no PA. Brand PREMPHASE and PREMPRO (conjugated estrogens/medroxyprogesterone) are Tier 2, no PA. DUAVEE (conjugated estrogens/bazedoxifene) and BIJUVA (bioidentical estradiol/progesterone) are both Tier 3, no PA. Targeted string search confirmed brand ACTIVELLA and ANGELIQ do not appear anywhere in the document -- fully generic-substituted. estradiol-norethindrone acetate 1mg/0.5mg chosen as the representative preferred agent per standard first-pass clinical guidance, matching the formulary's own lowest-tier/no-restriction placement. Cover page: 'Created: October 1, 2025'; content pages: 'Last Update: 06/15/26'.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the MGB Health Plan 2026 6-Tier Pharmacy Formulary PDF (fm.formularynavigator.com/FBO/192/20266T.pdf, header 'CURRENT AS OF 7/1/2026', PDF metadata mod date 2026-06-30). Confirmed: 'Estradiol Transdermal Patch Weekly' (brand ref Climara) = Tier 2, no restriction; 'Estradiol Transdermal Patch Twice Weekly' (brand ref Dotti) = Tier 2, no restriction; 'Estradiol Transdermal Gel 0.25/0.5/0.75/1 MG' (brand ref Divigel) = Tier 2, no restriction; 'Estradiol Transdermal Gel 0.75 MG/1.25 GM (0.06%)' (brand ref Estrogel) = Tier 2, no restriction. By contrast, 'Alora Transdermal Patch Twice Weekly' = Tier 4 with no PA/ST flag shown (higher-cost non-preferred brand tier, listed separately from the generic twice-weekly patch entry -- treated as non-formulary-preferred rather than 'alternative' since it is a distinct, more expensive brand with an equivalent generic already covered); 'Elestrin' = Tier 4; 'Climara Pro' (combination patch) = Tier 4; 'Evamist' (transdermal spray, no generic) = Tier 4 but is genuinely the only spray option so it is listed as a covered alternative, not a reject. Searched for 'Minivelle' and 'Vivelle' with zero matches across the full 134-page text -- confirmed absent (non-formulary), not a parsing gap. Generic weekly patch (Climara-equivalent) at Tier 2 was chosen as the representative preferred agent per usual first-line transdermal HT dosing.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the MGB Health Plan 2026 6-Tier Pharmacy Formulary PDF (fm.formularynavigator.com/FBO/192/20266T.pdf, header 'CURRENT AS OF 7/1/2026', PDF metadata mod date 2026-06-30). 'Estradiol Oral' is listed at Tier 1 with no PA/ST/QL flag -- the fully generic oral estradiol tablet is the plan's only standalone oral estrogen product. Searched exhaustively for 'Premarin' and 'conjugated' across the full 134-page extracted text: the only match is 'Premarin Vaginal' (Tier 3, a local vaginal cream, covered separately under the vaginal-estrogen class) -- there is no standalone oral Premarin tablet or generic conjugated-estrogens oral line anywhere in the document, so oral conjugated estrogens is marked nonformulary rather than fabricated as PA/step. No brand-name oral estradiol product (e.g. Estrace tablet) appears as its own line either, consistent with oral estradiol being fully generic. Because generic estradiol is the only oral estrogen-alone option on this formulary, there are no alternatives to list in this class beyond the single preferred agent.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the MGB Health Plan 2026 6-Tier Pharmacy Formulary PDF (fm.formularynavigator.com/FBO/192/20266T.pdf, header 'CURRENT AS OF 7/1/2026', PDF metadata mod date 2026-06-30). 'Progesterone Oral' (brand ref Prometrium) = Tier 2, no PA/ST/QL flag. 'medroxyPROGESTERone Acetate Oral' (brand ref Provera) = Tier 1, no restriction. 'Norethindrone Acetate Oral' (brand ref Gallifrey, a generic-name reference used by the FormularyNavigator tool, not a real trade name) = Tier 2, no restriction. Searched for 'Aygestin' with zero matches -- confirmed absent from the document (non-formulary), not a parsing gap. Micronized progesterone (Prometrium generic) was selected as the representative preferred agent per current clinical guidance favoring it for endometrial protection in patients on systemic estrogen with an intact uterus, even though the formulary itself does not explicitly designate one progestogen as preferred over another -- all three are similarly low-tier/unrestricted, so the clinical-preference ranking (not a formulary-stated ranking) is reflected in the ordering here.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the MGB Health Plan 2026 6-Tier Pharmacy Formulary PDF (fm.formularynavigator.com/FBO/192/20266T.pdf, header 'CURRENT AS OF 7/1/2026', PDF metadata mod date 2026-06-30). 'Zafemy' is listed at '$0' -- the plan's zero-dollar-copay tier per the document's own legend page (LEGEND: '$0 = Zero Dollar Copay') -- with no PA/ST/QL flag, making it the most favorably covered vaginal estrogen product on this formulary; this tier/restriction fact is read verbatim. 'Estradiol Vaginal Cream 0.01%' (brand ref Estrace) = Tier 2, no restriction. 'Estradiol Vaginal Tablet' (brand ref Yuvafem) = Tier 2, no restriction, and also appears again later in the alphabetical list under 'Yuvafem' at Tier 2 (duplicate listing, same product). 'Estring Vaginal Ring 7.5 MCG/24HR' = Tier 3, no PA/ST flag shown but a non-preferred tier vs. the $0/Tier 2 cream and insert options. 'Premarin Vaginal' = Tier 3, no restriction. Searched for 'Imvexxy' and 'Femring' with zero matches for either as standalone brand lines -- confirmed absent (non-formulary), not a parsing gap. Marked 'partial' rather than 'verified' because the formulary's own line item for Zafemy carries no strength/dosage detail at all (unlike most other entries in this document) -- the 4 mcg strength, insertion schedule, and sig in preferredAgent are inferred from Zafemy/Imvexxy's known FDA-approved dosing (external knowledge), not read off this source document. Zafemy was selected as the representative preferred agent given its $0 tier is the most favorable in the class, which IS a verbatim tier fact.

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the MGB Health Plan 2026 6-Tier Pharmacy Formulary PDF (fm.formularynavigator.com/FBO/192/20266T.pdf, header 'CURRENT AS OF 7/1/2026', PDF metadata mod date 2026-06-30). Three separate generic estradiol/norethindrone acetate combination lines were found, each listed under a different FormularyNavigator-assigned reference name (used by this tool for generic products lacking a marketed brand, not real trade names): 'Jinteli' = Tier 2, no restriction; 'Mimvey' = Tier 2, no restriction; 'Estradiol-Norethindrone Acet' (ref 'Abigale Lo') = Tier 2, no restriction. 'Prempro' (brand, conjugated estrogens/medroxyprogesterone continuous-combined) = Tier 3, no PA/ST flag shown. 'Premphase' (brand, same combination cyclic dosing) = Tier 3, no PA/ST flag shown. 'Bijuva' (bio-identical estradiol/progesterone, brand-only, no generic) = Tier 4 with an explicit 'PA' flag. Generic estradiol/norethindrone acetate at Tier 2 was chosen as the representative preferred agent per the task's own guidance that this combination is the usual first-pass generic option; the formulary does not explicitly rank the three Tier-2 generic-reference names against each other, so they are listed as interchangeable alternatives at the same tier.
